revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text: #redirect[[en:Takakuni_no_Hikono_Mikoto]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 武国彦命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 建大臣命、武大臣命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = 初代[[洲羽国造]] | 主君 = [[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = [[諏訪氏]]（洲羽国造） | 父母 = 父：[[真曽我男命]] | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = [[兄武彦命]]、[[弟武彦命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''武国彦命'''（たけくにひこのみこと、生没年不詳）とは、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[洲羽国造]]'''。'''建大臣命'''（たけおおおみのみこと）とも。
== 概要 == 別名に'''武大臣命'''<ref>[[中田憲信]]「諏訪氏」『諸系譜』、第十三冊。</ref>や、'''建大臣命'''<ref>[[延川和彦]]著、「諏訪氏系図」『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、25頁。</ref>があるとされる。
[[山梨県]][[北杜市]]に鎮座する諏訪神社中社や『甲斐国史』の伝承では、[[崇神天皇|崇神朝]]に[[建沼河別命]]が東征の功によって西域の地に封ぜられ、その孫の大臣命が諏訪国造に任命され、同地に廟を建てたとされる。また「[[国造本紀]]」の写本の一つにも[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に須羽国造に任命されたと見えるが、この記述は[[那須国造]]との誤記・混同と見る説がある一方<ref>佐藤雄一著『シナノの国造勢力』</ref><ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「第2章 皇族系氏族 第1節 多氏族 12神人部直、神人部宿祢」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年。</ref>、通常の写本に洲羽国造が見えないのは那須国造と混同されて記事が消滅した可能性も指摘されている<ref>「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/suwasya1.htm 諏訪社の奉斎氏族]」『古樹紀之房間』、2010年。</ref>。
なお多くの[[諏訪氏]]・[[神氏]]の系図では、初代[[洲羽国造]]は[[神功皇后|稚桜宮朝]]の兄武彦命とされる<ref>中田憲信「諏訪氏」『諸系譜』、第六冊。</ref><ref>中田憲信「神氏」『諸系譜』、第二十八冊。</ref>。
== 系譜 == 父は[[建御名方神]]の五世孫・会知早雄命の子である真曽我男命で、子に[[兄武彦命]]と[[木蘇国造]]の祖・[[弟武彦命]]がいる。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[国造]] *[[諏訪大社]] *[[信濃国]] *[[諏訪国]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:たけくにひこのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:諏訪氏]] [[Category:飛鳥時代以前の人物]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-stub}}

== 概要 == 別名に'''武大臣命'''<ref>[[中田憲信]]「諏訪氏」『諸系譜』、第十三冊。</ref>や、'''建大臣命'''<ref>[[延川和彦]]著、「諏訪氏系図」『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、25頁。</ref>があるとされる。
[[山梨県]][[北杜市]]に鎮座する諏訪神社中社や『甲斐国史』の伝承では、[[崇神天皇|崇神朝]]に[[建沼河別命]]が東征の功によって西域の地に封ぜられ、その孫の大臣命が諏訪国造に任命され、同地に廟を建てたとされる。また「[[国造本紀]]」の写本の一つにも[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に須羽国造に任命されたと見えるが、この記述は[[那須国造]]との誤記・混同と見る説がある<ref>佐藤雄一著『シナノの国造勢力』</ref><ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「第2章 皇族系氏族 第1節 多氏族 12神人部直、神人部宿祢」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年。</ref>。

revision.comment: [[Special:Contributions/おっふ|おっふ]] ([[User talk:おっふ|会話]]) による ID:89476796 の版を[[H:RV|取り消し]]唯の個人サイトではなく、20年以上も前から続く、宝賀寿男公認の古代氏族研究会公認ＨＰ（関連の日本家系図学会ですら、最近（2021年）やっと公式HPを持てたように、古代氏族研究会の公式HPはいまだなく、このHPが代理になっている）。まるで悪戯のように、このソースだけを狙って削除して回っている為、復帰)
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 武国彦命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 建大臣命、武大臣命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = 初代[[洲羽国造]] | 主君 = [[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = [[諏訪氏|洲羽君]]、[[神人部直]]遠祖 | 父母 = 父：[[真曽我男命]] | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = [[兄武彦命]]、[[弟武彦命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''武国彦命'''（たけくにひこのみこと、生没年不詳）とは、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[洲羽国造]]'''。'''建大臣命'''（たけおおおみのみこと）とも。
== 概要 == 別名に'''武大臣命'''<ref>[[中田憲信]]「諏訪氏」『諸系譜』、第十三冊。</ref>や、'''建大臣命'''<ref>[[延川和彦]]著、「諏訪氏系図」『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、25頁。</ref>があるとされる。
[[山梨県]][[北杜市]]に鎮座する諏訪神社中社や『甲斐国史』の伝承では、[[崇神天皇|崇神朝]]に[[建沼河別命]]が東征の功によって西域の地に封ぜられ、その孫の大臣命が諏訪国造に任命され、同地に廟を建てたとされる。また「[[国造本紀]]」の写本の一つにも[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に須羽国造に任命されたと見えるが、この記述は[[那須国造]]との誤記・混同と見る説がある一方<ref>佐藤雄一著『シナノの国造勢力』</ref><ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「第2章 皇族系氏族 第1節 多氏族 12神人部直、神人部宿祢」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年。</ref>、通常の写本に洲羽国造が見えないのは那須国造と混同されて記事が消滅した可能性も指摘されている<ref>「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/suwasya1.htm 諏訪社の奉斎氏族]」『古樹紀之房間』、2010年。</ref>。
なお多くの[[諏訪氏]]・[[神氏]]の系図では、初代[[洲羽国造]]は[[神功皇后|稚桜宮朝]]の兄武彦命とされる<ref>中田憲信「諏訪氏」『諸系譜』、第六冊。</ref><ref>中田憲信「神氏」『諸系譜』、第二十八冊。</ref>。
== 系譜 == 父は[[建御名方神]]の五世孫・会知早雄命の子である真曽我男命で、子に[[兄武彦命]]と[[木蘇国造]]の祖・[[弟武彦命]]がいる。

== 関連項目 == *[[国造]] *[[諏訪大社]] *[[信濃国]] *[[諏訪国]] *[[八縣宿禰神]] *[[武美名別命]] *[[健隈照命]] *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:たけくにひこのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:国造主]] [[Category:信濃国の人物]] [[Category:諏訪氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[Category:長野県の歴史]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-stub}}

revision.comment: 戻し：「国造主」という学術用語なし。勝手な造語につき。
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 武国彦命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 建大臣命、武大臣命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = }} '''武国彦命'''（たけくにひこのみこと、生没年不詳）とは、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]。'''建大臣命'''（たけおおおみのみこと）とも。
== 概要 == 別名に'''武大臣命'''<ref>[[中田憲信]]「諏訪氏」『諸系譜』、第十三冊。</ref>や、'''建大臣命'''<ref>[[延川和彦]]著、「諏訪氏系図」『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、25頁。</ref>があるとされる。
[[山梨県]][[北杜市]]に鎮座する諏訪神社中社や『甲斐国史』の伝承では、[[崇神天皇|崇神朝]]に[[建沼河別命]]が東征の功によって西域の地に封ぜられ、その孫の大臣命が諏訪国造に任命され、同地に廟を建てたとされる。また「[[国造本紀]]」の写本の一つにも[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に須羽国造に任命されたと見えるが、この記述は[[那須国造]]との誤記・混同と見る説がある<ref>佐藤雄一著『シナノの国造勢力』</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[国造]] *[[諏訪大社]] *[[信濃国]] *[[諏訪国]] *[[八縣宿禰神]] *[[武美名別命]] *[[健隈照命]] *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:たけくにひこのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:信濃国の人物]] [[Category:諏訪氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[Category:長野県の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Takekunihiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Takedainin no Mikoto, Takeōomi no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = }}
'''Takekunihiko no Mikoto''' (武国彦命, birth and death dates unknown) was a [[Kofun period]] noble. He is also known as '''Takedainin no Mikoto''' (建大臣命) or '''Takeōomi no Mikoto''' (武大臣命).
== Overview == Takekunihiko no Mikoto is referred to by alternate names such as '''Takeōomi no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Nakata Norinobu|ja|中田憲信}}. "諏訪氏", in 『諸系譜』, Vol. 13.</ref> and '''Takedainin no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Enkawa Kazuhiko|ja|延川和彦}}, "諏訪氏系図," in 『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』,飯田好太郎, 1921, p. 25.</ref>.
According to the traditions of the Suwa Shrine's Middle Shrine in {{ill|Hokuto, Yamanashi|ja|北杜市}}, and the accounts in 『甲斐国史』, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, {{ill|Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|ja|建沼河別命}} was granted lands in the western region for his contributions during the eastern expedition. His grandson, Dainin no Mikoto, was reportedly appointed as the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} of Suwa Province and established a shrine there.
In a manuscript of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|ja|国造本紀}}, there is mention of his appointment as Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, although some theories suggest this might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|那須国造}}.<ref>Yuichi Sato, 『シナノの国造勢力』.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} * {{ill|Suwa Province|ja|諏訪国}} * {{ill|Yagata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}} * {{ill|Takeminawake no Mikoto|ja|武美名別命}} * {{ill|Takekumateru no Mikoto|ja|健隈照命}} * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

{{Infobox Noble | name = Takekunihiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Takedainin no Mikoto, Takeōomi no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = }}
'''Takekunihiko no Mikoto''' (武国彦命, birth and death dates unknown) was a [[Kofun period]] noble. He is also known as '''Takedainin no Mikoto''' (建大臣命) or '''Takeōomi no Mikoto''' (武大臣命).
== Overview == Takekunihiko no Mikoto is referred to by alternate names such as '''Takeōomi no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Nakata Norinobu|ja|中田憲信}}. "諏訪氏", in 『諸系譜』, Vol. 13.</ref> and '''Takedainin no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Enkawa Kazuhiko|ja|延川和彦}}, "諏訪氏系図," in 『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』,飯田好太郎, 1921, p. 25.</ref>.
According to the traditions of the Suwa Shrine's Middle Shrine in {{ill|Hokuto, Yamanashi|ja|北杜市}}, and the accounts in 『甲斐国史』, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, {{ill|Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|ja|建沼河別命}} was granted lands in the western region for his contributions during the eastern expedition. His grandson, Dainin no Mikoto, was reportedly appointed as the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} of Suwa Province and established a shrine there.
In a manuscript of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|ja|国造本紀}}, there is mention of his appointment as Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, although some theories suggest this might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|那須国造}}.<ref>Yuichi Sato, 『シナノの国造勢力』.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} * {{ill|Suwa Province|ja|諏訪国}} * {{ill|Yagata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}} * {{ill|Takeminawake no Mikoto|ja|武美名別命}} * {{ill|Takekumateru no Mikoto|ja|健隈照命}} * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Takekunihiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Takedainin no Mikoto, Takeōomi no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = }}
'''Takekunihiko no Mikoto''' (武国彦命, birth and death dates unknown) was a [[Kofun period]] noble. He is also known as '''Takedainin no Mikoto''' (建大臣命) or '''Takeōomi no Mikoto''' (武大臣命).
== Overview == Takekunihiko no Mikoto is referred to by alternate names such as '''Takeōomi no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Nakata Norinobu|ja|中田憲信|12=draft}}. "諏訪氏", in 『諸系譜』, Vol. 13.</ref> and '''Takedainin no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Enkawa Kazuhiko|ja|延川和彦|12=draft}}, "諏訪氏系図," in 『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』,飯田好太郎, 1921, p. 25.</ref>.
According to the traditions of the Suwa Shrine's Middle Shrine in {{ill|Hokuto, Yamanashi|ja|北杜市|12=draft}}, and the accounts in 『甲斐国史』, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|ja|建沼河別命|12=draft}} was granted lands in the western region for his contributions during the eastern expedition. His grandson, Dainin no Mikoto, was reportedly appointed as the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft}} of Suwa Province and established a shrine there.
In a manuscript of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=draft}}, there is mention of his appointment as Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}}, although some theories suggest this might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|那須国造|12=draft}}.<ref>Yuichi Sato, 『シナノの国造勢力』.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suwa Province|ja|諏訪国|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yagata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takeminawake no Mikoto|ja|武美名別命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takekumateru no Mikoto|ja|健隈照命|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-stub}}
<!--Takakuni no Hikono Mikoto--> <!--Takakuni no Hikono Mikoto-->

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 武国彦命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 建大臣命、武大臣命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = }} '''武国彦命'''（たけくにひこのみこと、生没年不詳）とは、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]。'''建大臣命'''（たけおおおみのみこと）とも。
== 概要 == 別名に'''武大臣命'''<ref>[[中田憲信]]「諏訪氏」『諸系譜』、第十三冊。</ref>や、'''建大臣命'''<ref>[[延川和彦]]著、「諏訪氏系図」『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、25頁。</ref>があるとされる。
[[山梨県]][[北杜市]]に鎮座する諏訪神社中社や『甲斐国史』の伝承では、[[崇神天皇|崇神朝]]に[[建沼河別命]]が東征の功によって西域の地に封ぜられ、その孫の大臣命が諏訪国造に任命され、同地に廟を建てたとされる。また「[[国造本紀]]」の写本の一つにも[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に須羽国造に任命されたと見えるが、この記述は[[那須国造]]との誤記・混同と見る説がある<ref>佐藤雄一著『シナノの国造勢力』</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[国造]] *[[諏訪大社]] *[[信濃国]] *[[諏訪国]] *[[八縣宿禰神]] *[[武美名別命]] *[[健隈照命]] *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:たけくにひこのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:信濃国の人物]] [[Category:諏訪氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[Category:長野県の歴史]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:武国彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q100453594]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Takekunihiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Takedainin no Mikoto, Takeōomi no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = }}
'''Takekunihiko no Mikoto''' (武国彦命, birth and death dates unknown) was a [[Kofun period]] noble. He is also known as '''Takedainin no Mikoto''' (建大臣命) or '''Takeōomi no Mikoto''' (武大臣命).
== Overview == Takekunihiko no Mikoto is referred to by alternate names such as '''Takeōomi no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Nakata Norinobu|ja|中田憲信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakata Norinobu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491601}}. "諏訪氏", in 『諸系譜』, Vol. 13.</ref> and '''Takedainin no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Enkawa Kazuhiko|ja|延川和彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkawa Kazuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "諏訪氏系図," in 『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』,飯田好太郎, 1921, p. 25.</ref>.
According to the traditions of the Suwa Shrine's Middle Shrine in [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto, Yamanashi]], and the accounts in 『甲斐国史』, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|lt=Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|lt=Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|ja|建沼河別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}} was granted lands in the western region for his contributions during the eastern expedition. His grandson, Dainin no Mikoto, was reportedly appointed as the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of Suwa Province and established a shrine there.
In a manuscript of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}, there is mention of his appointment as Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], although some theories suggest this might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|那須国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.<ref>Yuichi Sato, 『シナノの国造勢力』.</ref>

'''Takekunihiko no Mikoto''' (武国彦命, birth and death dates unknown) was a [[Kofun period]] noble. He is also known as '''Takedainin no Mikoto''' (建大臣命) or '''Takeōomi no Mikoto''' (武大臣命).
== Overview == Takekunihiko no Mikoto is referred to by alternate names such as '''Takeōomi no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Nakata Norinobu|ja|中田憲信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakata Norinobu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491601}}. "諏訪氏", in 『諸系譜』, Vol. 13.</ref> and '''Takedainin no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Enkawa Kazuhiko|ja|延川和彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkawa Kazuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "諏訪氏系図," in 『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』,飯田好太郎, 1921, p. 25.</ref>.
According to the traditions of the Suwa Shrine's Middle Shrine in [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto, Yamanashi]], and the accounts in 『甲斐国史』, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|lt=Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|lt=Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|ja|建沼河別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}} was granted lands in the western region for his contributions during the eastern expedition. His grandson, Dainin no Mikoto, was reportedly appointed as the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of Suwa Province and established a shrine there.
In a manuscript of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}, there is mention of his appointment as Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], although some theories suggest this might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|那須国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.<ref>Yuichi Sato, 『シナノの国造勢力』.</ref>

In a manuscript of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}, there is mention of his appointment as Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], although some theories suggest this might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|那須国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.<ref>Yuichi Sato, 『シナノの国造勢力』.</ref>

'''Takekunihiko no Mikoto''' (武国彦命, birth and death dates unknown) was a [[Kofun period]] noble. He is also known as '''Takedainin no Mikoto''' (建大臣命) or '''Takeōomi no Mikoto''' (武大臣命).
== Overview == Takekunihiko no Mikoto is referred to by alternate names such as '''Takeōomi no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Nakata Norinobu|ja|中田憲信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakata Norinobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491601}}. "諏訪氏", in 『諸系譜』, Vol. 13.</ref> and '''Takedainin no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Enkawa Kazuhiko|ja|延川和彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkawa Kazuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "諏訪氏系図," in 『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』,飯田好太郎, 1921, p. 25.</ref>.

His grandson, Dainin no Mikoto, was reportedly appointed as the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of Suwa Province and established a shrine there.
In a manuscript of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}, there is mention of his appointment as Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], although some theories suggest this might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|那須国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.<ref>Yuichi Sato, 『シナノの国造勢力』.</ref>

In a manuscript of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}, there is mention of his appointment as Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], although some theories suggest this might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|那須国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.<ref>Yuichi Sato, 『シナノの国造勢力』.</ref>

'''Takekunihiko no Mikoto''' (武国彦命, birth and death dates unknown) was a [[Kofun period]] noble. He is also known as '''Takedainin no Mikoto''' (建大臣命) or '''Takeōomi no Mikoto''' (武大臣命).
== Overview == Takekunihiko no Mikoto is referred to by alternate names such as '''Takeōomi no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Nakata Norinobu|ja|中田憲信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakata Norinobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491601}}. "諏訪氏", in 『諸系譜』, Vol. 13.</ref> and '''Takedainin no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Enkawa Kazuhiko|ja|延川和彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkawa Kazuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "諏訪氏系図," in 『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』,飯田好太郎, 1921, p. 25.</ref>.
According to the traditions of the Suwa Shrine's Middle Shrine in [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto, Yamanashi]], and the accounts in 『甲斐国史』, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|lt=Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|lt=Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|ja|建沼河別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}} was granted lands in the western region for his contributions during the eastern expedition. His grandson, Dainin no Mikoto, was reportedly appointed as the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of Suwa Province and established a shrine there.
In a manuscript of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}, there is mention of his appointment as Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], although some theories suggest this might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|那須国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.<ref>Yuichi Sato, 『シナノの国造勢力』.</ref>

According to the traditions of the Suwa Shrine's Middle Shrine in [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto, Yamanashi]], and the accounts in 『甲斐国史』, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|lt=Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|lt=Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|ja|建沼河別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}} was granted lands in the western region for his contributions during the eastern expedition. His grandson, Dainin no Mikoto, was reportedly appointed as the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of Suwa Province and established a shrine there.
In a manuscript of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}, there is mention of his appointment as Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], although some theories suggest this might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|那須国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.<ref>Yuichi Sato, 『シナノの国造勢力』.</ref>

'''Takekunihiko no Mikoto''' (武国彦命, birth and death dates unknown) was a [[Kofun period]] noble. He is also known as '''Takedainin no Mikoto''' (建大臣命) or '''Takeōomi no Mikoto''' (武大臣命).
== Overview == Takekunihiko no Mikoto is referred to by alternate names such as '''Takeōomi no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Nakata Norinobu|ja|中田憲信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakata Norinobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491601}}. "諏訪氏", in 『諸系譜』, Vol. 13.</ref> and '''Takedainin no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Enkawa Kazuhiko|ja|延川和彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkawa Kazuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "諏訪氏系図," in 『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』,飯田好太郎, 1921, p. 25.</ref>.
According to the traditions of the Suwa Shrine's Middle Shrine in [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto, Yamanashi]], and the accounts in 『甲斐国史』, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|lt=Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|lt=Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|ja|建沼河別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}} was granted lands in the western region for his contributions during the eastern expedition. His grandson, Dainin no Mikoto, was reportedly appointed as the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of Suwa Province and established a shrine there.
In a manuscript of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}, there is mention of his appointment as Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], although some theories suggest this might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|那須国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.<ref>Yuichi Sato, 『シナノの国造勢力』.</ref>

'''Takekunihiko no Mikoto''' (武国彦命, birth and death dates unknown) was a [[Kofun period]] noble. He is also known as '''Takedainin no Mikoto''' (建大臣命) or '''Takeōomi no Mikoto''' (武大臣命).
== Overview == Takekunihiko no Mikoto is referred to by alternate names such as '''Takeōomi no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Nakata Norinobu|ja|中田憲信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakata Norinobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491601}}. "諏訪氏", in 『諸系譜』, Vol. 13.</ref> and '''Takedainin no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Enkawa Kazuhiko|ja|延川和彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkawa Kazuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "諏訪氏系図," in 『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』,飯田好太郎, 1921, p. 25.</ref>.
According to the traditions of the Suwa Shrine's Middle Shrine in [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto, Yamanashi]], and the accounts in 『甲斐国史』, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|lt=Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|lt=Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|ja|建沼河別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}} was granted lands in the western region for his contributions during the eastern expedition. His grandson, Dainin no Mikoto, was reportedly appointed as the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of Suwa Province and established a shrine there.
In a manuscript of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}, there is mention of his appointment as Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], although some theories suggest this might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|那須国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.<ref>Yuichi Sato, 『シナノの国造勢力』.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] * {{ill|Suwa Province|ja|諏訪国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏方国|1=Suwa Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1192248}} * {{ill|Yagata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagata no Sukune no Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Takeminawake no Mikoto|ja|武美名別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminawake no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Takekumateru no Mikoto|ja|健隈照命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekumateru no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of figures before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|武国彦命|version=99874848|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:武国彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:武国彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:武国彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}} {{Infobox noble | name = Takekunihiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Takedainin no Mikoto, Takeōomi no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = }}
'''Takekunihiko no Mikoto''' (武国彦命, birth and death dates unknown) was a [[Kofun period]] noble. He is also known as '''Takedainin no Mikoto''' (建大臣命) or '''Takeōomi no Mikoto''' (武大臣命).
== Overview == Takekunihiko no Mikoto is referred to by alternate names such as '''Takeōomi no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Nakata Norinobu|ja|中田憲信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakata Norinobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491601}}. "諏訪氏", in 『諸系譜』, Vol. 13.</ref> and '''Takedainin no Mikoto'''<ref>{{ill|Enkawa Kazuhiko|ja|延川和彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkawa Kazuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, "諏訪氏系図," in 『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』,飯田好太郎, 1921, p. 25.</ref>.
According to the traditions of the Suwa Shrine's Middle Shrine in {{ill|Hokuto, Yamanashi|en|Hokuto, Yamanashi|WD=Q825272|lt=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, and the accounts in 『甲斐国史』, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, {{ill|Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|lt=Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|lt=Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|ja|建沼河別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenumakawabete no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別}} was granted lands in the western region for his contributions during the eastern expedition. His grandson, Dainin no Mikoto, was reportedly appointed as the {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko}} of Suwa Province and established a shrine there.
In a manuscript of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}, there is mention of his appointment as Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}, although some theories suggest this might be a mistaken reference to {{ill|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|那須国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.<ref>Yuichi Sato, 『シナノの国造勢力』.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko}} * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} * {{ill|Suwa Province|ja|諏訪国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏方国|1=Suwa Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1192248}} * {{ill|Yagata no Sukune no Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagata no Sukune no Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Takeminawake no Mikoto|ja|武美名別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminawake no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Takekumateru no Mikoto|ja|健隈照命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekumateru no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of figures before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|武国彦命|version=99874848|comment=Imported full ja history}}
